info.name: TMJConnect API
info.description:
HIPAA-compliant orofacial pain management platform API.
Supports patients, providers, and admin roles.


Contact Support:
 Name: TMJConnect Engineering

request.description: Flow: register → verify-email → tokens (no MFA)

response.name: Account created. Check email for a 6-digit verification code that expires after 5 minutes.

request.description:
Returns tokens directly if MFA is not enabled.
If patient has opted into MFA, returns mfa_required + mfa_token instead.
Use /auth/mfa/verify to complete login in that case.

response.name: WRONG_PORTAL (provider trying patient login), VERIFY_EMAIL, ACCOUNT_INACTIVE, or ACCOUNT_DELETED. ACCOUNT_DELETED means the 24-hour self-restore window has passed and the user can submit /auth/account-restore/request.

request.description:
Returns a setup_token. Use it with the existing MFA endpoints:
1. POST /auth/mfa/setup (Bearer: setup_token) → QR code + secret
2. POST /auth/mfa/verify-setup (Bearer: setup_token + TOTP code) → backup codes + tokens

request.description:
Requires current password for verification. Clears TOTP secret,
disables MFA flag, and deletes all backup codes. Providers cannot
disable MFA — this endpoint returns 403 for non-patient roles.

request.description: Flow: register → verify-email → mfa/setup → mfa/verify-setup → tokens + backup codes

response.name: Account created. Check email for a 6-digit verification code that expires after 5 minutes.

item.name: Provider login (always returns mfa_token, MFA is mandatory)

request.description:
Providers must complete MFA on every login. Returns an mfa_token
which must be passed to /auth/mfa/verify with a TOTP, SMS, or backup code.
Returns 403 MFA_NOT_SETUP if the provider has not completed onboarding.

response.name: WRONG_PORTAL (patient trying provider login), MFA_NOT_SETUP, VERIFY_EMAIL, ACCOUNT_INACTIVE, or ACCOUNT_DELETED. ACCOUNT_DELETED means the 24-hour self-restore window has passed and the user can submit /auth/account-restore/request.

item.name: Begin MFA setup (provider onboarding, after verify-email)

request.description:
Called once during provider onboarding. Requires the setup_token
returned by /auth/verify-email in the Authorization header.
Returns the TOTP secret and QR URI for authenticator app setup.

response.name: TOTP secret + QR URI returned for authenticator app

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Complete MFA setup with first TOTP code (issues tokens)

request.description:
Verifies the provider's first TOTP code, enables MFA, generates 10 backup codes,
and issues access_token + refresh_token so the provider is logged in immediately.

response.name: MFA enabled. Returns backup codes + tokens.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Verify MFA code to complete provider login

request.description: Accepts TOTP code, a 5-minute SMS OTP, or one-time backup code.
Generates a random one-time SMS code that expires after 5 minutes. Requesting a new code replaces the previous SMS code.

Shared by both patient and provider registration flows.
- Patient: returns access_token + refresh_token (logged in immediately).
- Provider: returns mfa_setup_required + setup_token (must complete MFA setup).

response.name: Patient gets tokens; provider gets setup_token for MFA onboarding
Too many incorrect attempts — code invalidated
Always 200 (never reveals if email exists). Sends a 5-minute 6-digit OTP if account exists.

item.name: Verify 5-minute password reset OTP and receive short-lived reset token
Complete password reset using short-lived reset token

response.name: Always 200. Rate limited to 1 per 2 minutes.

item.name: Request admin review to restore a deleted account

request.description:
Public endpoint used after login returns `ACCOUNT_DELETED`. The response is intentionally generic
and does not reveal whether an email exists. If the email belongs to a soft-deleted user and there
is no pending request, a pending account restore request is queued for admin review.

response.name: Request accepted generically

item.name: Request to change the account email address

request.description:
Step 1 of the two-step email change flow. Verifies the current password,
then emails a 5-minute 6-digit verification code to the proposed new address.
Email is only updated after /auth/change-email/verify is called with
that code.

- `active` - Only active assignments
- `completed` - Only completed assignments
- `paused` - Only paused assignments
- Omit `status` - Return all assignments regardless of status
